lessons.title: Multiple Elements Match — Locator Not Specific Enough
lessons.context: Playwright runs in strict mode by default — if a locator matches more than one element, it throws a 'strict mode violation' error. This forces tests to be explicit about which element they intend to interact with, preventing subtle bugs from acting on the wrong element.

problem.error: strict mode violation: locator resolved to N elements
problem.pattern: strict mode violation|multiple elements|resolved to \d+ elements
problem.cause: Locator matches multiple elements on the page — not specific enough to identify a single target

antipattern.description: Using a generic locator that matches multiple elements
antipattern.why_bad: Playwright strict mode throws an error — acting on the wrong element causes silent test bugs
code: // BAD — matches all buttons with text 'Submit'
await page.getByText('Submit').click(); // Fails if multiple 'Submit' buttons exist

solution.description: Use getByTestId for unique identification, or add filter() to narrow down to the specific element.
code: // GOOD — filter by visible text in a list

lessons.ai_instruction: When a locator might match multiple elements, use getByTestId() for unique elements, or chain .filter({ hasText }) or parent context locators to narrow the match to exactly one element.
